claim: Agents require 2,000 hours of practice before users can reliably predict their behavior and trust them—the learning curve is steep and unavoidable
actionable: Allocate at least a year of active agent use to develop proper mental models; don't judge capability from 2-hour trials alone
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Senior engineers (12–15 years experience) experience the most resistance to AI-assisted coding because their identity is tied to traditional workflows
actionable: When managing senior engineers through AI adoption, acknowledge identity shift and frame vibe coding as evolution, not replacement
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Never anthropomorphize LLMs—they are unpredictable tools that can betray expectations (delete production databases) even after appearing to understand your goals
actionable: Assume LLMs will make catastrophic mistakes; isolate critical access and use safeguards regardless of apparent competence history
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: The next frontier after IDE-centric coding is agent orchestration dashboards where developers manage AI agent fleets like NASCAR pit crews
actionable: Start thinking of your development role as workflow orchestration and triage, not code writing; build or adopt orchestration dashboards
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Merging is emerging as the bottleneck when 10x productivity gains are achieved—agents make 30k-line changes simultaneously, creating complex integration problems
actionable: Plan for merge strategies (stack diffs, queuing, file reservations) before deploying multiple high-velocity agents on the same codebase
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Open-source models are 7 months behind frontier models but converging fast; they will match Gemini-3 capability within 12–18 months
actionable: Invest in OSS model infrastructure now; plan cost optimization around smaller models for non-critical tasks within next 18 months
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: When using agents, diff review by shape and color (not full code reading) can reveal correctness issues—expertise develops patterns for fast triage
actionable: Train yourself to recognize red flags in diffs: unusual shapes, excessive line counts, or suspicious patterns; use this as fast pre-review filter
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Multiple agents in a shared repo with file reservations (like Beads) can work reliably if the system automatically resolves conflicts when issues arise
actionable: Implement file reservation + auto-repair loops if running many agents in one repo; this works because agents are good at fixing their own messes
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: The entire tech stack of 2024 (Claude Code, Cursor) is already obsolete; real future is in orchestrators and multi-agent workflows
actionable: Don't optimize too heavily for individual-agent tools; plan architecture for agent teams and orchestration instead
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: You should stop writing code by hand entirely in high-productivity setups—all new code starts with prompts; manual edits become rare
actionable: Shift workflow to always start with agent generation; reserve manual coding for review, validation, and edge cases only
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE

claim: Rewriting entire codebases from scratch with LLMs often beats refactoring in both speed and quality—Joel Spolsky's 'never rewrite' is now antiquated
actionable: When refactoring large chunks becomes complex, evaluate full rewrites with agents as a faster, cleaner alternative
source_title: Steve Yegge's Vibe Coding Manifesto: Why Claude Code Isn't It & What Comes After the IDE
